title: From вҖңI Blew ItвҖқ to вҖңIвҖҷm LearningвҖқ: Stopping the Shame Spiral | Alcohol Freedom Coaching | EP 883
summary: Stopping the shame spiralвҖ”what if the fastest way out isnвҖҷt more willpower, but more compassion? In this episode of Alcohol Freedom Coaching, Coach Hayley supports Carol, a newly retired grandmother who felt вҖңso far behindвҖқ after a vacation slip, and Coach Cole guides Nancy, a devoted mom caring for a daughter with complex special needs, whoвҖҷs learning to relax without wine and let go of вҖңI should be doing moreвҖқ guilt. Together, they model how to shift from self-blame to curiosity, build emotion-based goals (happiness, energy, motivation), and reconnect with support inside The Path.  In CarolвҖҷs session: Naming the belief: вҖңIвҖҷm so far behindвҖқ vs. вҖңIвҖҷm exactly where IвҖҷm meant to be.вҖқ Turning slips into data pointsвҖ”not identityвҖ”and choosing curiosity over criticism. Building emotion-based goals: happiness, energy, motivation as a north star. Breaking overwhelm: 15-minute re-entry (one video, one call, one journal prompt). Navigating travel and social events without defaulting to comparison or shame. Finding connection again: taking up space on calls even when you feel вҖңnot bad enough.вҖқ Practical reframe for 3 a.m. thoughts and night-after guilt. Tools for stopping the shame spiral after a vacation or wedding slip .вҖҰand more. In NancyвҖҷs session: Spotting guilt in the body (chest/shoulders tightness) and softening with micro-moments. Permission slips: letting older siblings help, one small experiment at a time. Replacing the 4 p.m. wine ritual with soothing, sensory evening routines. Distinguishing healthy guilt (course-correct) from toxic shame (self-attack). Creating a вҖңtreatвҖқ that isnвҖҷt alcohol: porch time, breathwork, a few pages of a novel. Scripts to ask for help without apologizing for your needs.How curiosity interrupts ruminating about вҖңnot doing enough.вҖқ Practical ideas for stopping the shame spiral when responsibilities feel nonstop .вҖҰand more. Hayley Scherders is a certified TNM Coach with training from the Canadian Addiction and Mental Health Association. Drawing from personal experiences, Hayley understands how tough change can be and provides a safe, compassionate, and judgment-free space where her clients can feel supported. She believes that with the right mindset, anyone can change their life at any time. Learn more about Coach Hayley: https://thisnakedmind.com/coach/hayley-scherders/ Cole Harvey is a certified Naked Mind Senior Coach. For years, he felt lost and used alcohol as a way to cope, until he decided to go alcohol-free and focus on finding his purpose. Through curiosity, self-compassion, and adventure, he transformed his life. As a habit change and mindset coach, Cole helps young men understand themselves, build better habits, and find meaning. Learn more about Coach Cole: https://thisnakedmind.com/coach/cole-harvey/ Episode links: nakedmindpath.com Related Episodes: How can I not feel guilty for all the time I spent trying to fix myself? - Reader Question - E240 - https://thisnakedmind.com/ep-240-reader-question-how-can-i-not-feel-guilty-for-all-the-time-i-spent-trying-to-fix-myself/ В How to Explore Drinking Without Judgment - Alcohol Freedom Coaching - E813 - https://thisnakedmind.com/how-to-explore-drinking-without-judgment-alcohol-freedom-coaching-e813/ Ready to take the next step on your journey?В  Visit https://learn.thisnakedmind.com/podcast-resources for free resources, programs, and more. title: 50 - Power Reveals Character | The Hidden Cost of Growth
summary: What if the biggest problem in your business isnвҖҷt strategy, competition, or market conditions; but you? In Episode 50 of the Impact Podcast, Eddie Wilson explores one of the most uncomfortable truths in leadership: your business is a mirror. As you grow, scale, and experience pressure, your company doesnвҖҷt just generate revenue, it exposes emotional maturity, integrity, ego, and blind spots. Through historical examples, hard leadership questions, and direct reflection, Eddie challenges entrepreneurs to stop smashing the mirror and start refining the person staring back at them. If youвҖҷve ever wondered why growth feels harder at the top, why pressure changes people, or why scaling exposes weaknesses you didnвҖҷt know you had, this episode will shift how you see leadership forever. Because the real question isnвҖҷt вҖңHow do I fix the business?вҖқ ItвҖҷs вҖңWhat is the business asking me to fix in myself?вҖқ TIMESTAMPS: 00:00 вҖ” Start00:21 вҖ” The mirror you never asked for01:07 вҖ” Money as a revealer of character02:19 вҖ” Your business is the greatest mirror03:03 вҖ” Pressure reveals emotional maturity03:52 вҖ” Calm under pressure isnвҖҷt personality, itвҖҷs preparation04:18 вҖ” Scale reveals integrity05:41 вҖ” Growth reveals ego06:28 вҖ” Ego doesnвҖҷt disappear with success07:10 вҖ” Napoleon: systems, scale, and unchecked ego10:12 вҖ” Power doesnвҖҷt ruin you, it reveals you11:14 вҖ” Four leadership mirror questions12:19 вҖ” Avoidance is leadership debt13:49 вҖ” Who are you becoming under pressure?15:27 вҖ” Who do you want to be instead?16:23 вҖ” Your business is not your enemy, itвҖҷs your greatest teacher18:10 вҖ” The business grows to the level of the exposed leader  Hosted on Acast. title: Leap Beyond Limits: The Ilana Golan Story
summary: What if adversity isnвҖҷt the end of your story вҖ” but the launchpad? On this powerful episode of Women Road Warriors, Shelley Johnson and Kathy Tuccaro sit down with Ilana Golan вҖ” trailblazer, tech executive, Ironman triathlete, and founder of one of the fastest-growing companies in America вҖ“ Leap Academy. Ilana made history as the first woman to become a commander in the F-16 Flight Simulator in IsraelвҖҷs Air Force. She later became IntelвҖҷs youngest hire and rose through the ranks of Silicon Valley as a high-level tech executive. But her journey didnвҖҷt follow a straight line. After a devastating startup betrayal that left her at rock bottom, Ilana rebuilt вҖ” stronger, smarter, and unstoppable. Today, she is the CEO and founder of Leap Academy, ranked in the top 7% of the fastest-growing companies in the U.S., revolutionizing how we think about careers, education, and the future of work. She has interviewed icons like Richard Branson, Gary Vaynerchuk, and the president of Starbucks on her hit podcast and delivered keynotes on some of the worldвҖҷs biggest stages. This conversation dives into resilience, reinvention, leadership, and the courage to leap when life forces you to pivot. If youвҖҷve ever felt stuck, blindsided, or unsure of your next move вҖ” this episode will show you whatвҖҷs possible. ItвҖҷs not about avoiding adversity. title: Discover Your Working Genius and Beat Career Burnout | Patrick Lencioni | E147
summary: The world tells leaders they must be everything: visionary, innovator, operator, executor. Patrick Lencioni tried, but it nearly crushed him. He wasnвҖҷt exhausted from effort; he was exhausted from misalignment. He discovered what most high performers never admit: trying to be everything is the fastest path to burnout. That insight became The Six Types of Working Genius, a simple framework reshaping how teams work by aligning people with the work that gives them energy. In this episode, Patrick joins Ilana to break down the Six Types of Working Genius and show how aligning your strengths can transform your career, your team, and your fulfillment at work. Patrick Lencioni is a bestselling author and organizational health expert, and the founder of The Table Group. Named by Fortune as вҖңone of the new gurus you should know,вҖқ Patrick helps leaders build healthy, high-performing organizations grounded in trust and clarity. In this episode, Ilana and Patrick will discuss: (00:00) IntroductionВ  (02:46) PatrickвҖҷs Early Life and Career Beginnings (08:31) Joining Oracle and Pitching Ideas (12:36) Turning Down a Job Offer from Steve Jobs (15:40) The Decision to Start the Table Group (20:18) Navigating the Hard Moments in Entrepreneurship (23:30) Short Attention Spans as a Bestselling AuthorВ  (28:06) The Birth and Impact of Working GeniusВ  (34:48) Applying Working Genius to Career Choices (41:05) W-I-D-G-E-T: The Six Types of Working Genius (54:57) Healing Childhood Scars and Understanding Self (59:15) Q&A: The Keys to Building a Portfolio Career Patrick Lencioni is a bestselling author and organizational health expert, and the founder of The Table Group. He has written 13 books with over 7 million copies sold worldwide, including The Five Dysfunctions of a Team. He is also the creator of The 6 Types of Working Genius, a framework that helps teams identify the work that energizes them and improves performance. Named by Fortune as вҖңone of the new gurus you should know,вҖқ Patrick helps leaders build healthy, high-performing organizations grounded in trust and clarity. title: Today Is A Good Day To Die, part 2.
summary: Start Your Transformation Now In this follow-up episode of The Jim Fortin Podcast, Jim Fortin returns to the provocative theme of ego death introduced in Part 1, this time going deeper into the mechanics of ego identification and why most people remain trapped inside it. Drawing from shamanic teachings, brain science, and deeply personal storiesвҖ”including the moment a doctor told him he was in heart failureвҖ”Jim explains that fear is not a personal flaw but the energy of separation, a byproduct of the ego. Every limitation, every story of "I'm not good enough," every belief system that keeps us small, is rooted in ego. And all of it, Jim insists, was learned. Jim distinguishes between transformation and transcendence, sharing why he is now calling himself and his listeners toward something even deeper: moving beyond the identity of ego altogether. He revisits the habits of the mind that keep the ego alive, explains why love itself gets hijacked by ego, and offers a powerful shamanic reframeвҖ”that the soul is the witness, the parent watching the child, the light that dissolves the darkness of the ego. The episode also introduces Soul Forge, Jim's new program designed to help people identify and dissolve the ego's grip on their subconscious patterns.В  If Part 1 opened the door, Part 2 walks you through it. Listen and let this one sit with you.В  What YouвҖҷll Discover in This Episode:В  (02:53) Fear is not personalвҖ”it's the energy of separation Why every fear you entertain is ego-based, not soul-based, and how understanding this distinction begins to dissolve fear's power over your life.В  (05:47) The habit of knowing yourself as ego How we become so deeply conditioned to identify as our ego that we forget there's a soulful being underneathвҖ”and why recognition is the first step to breaking the cycle.В  (09:13) Identity, ego, and the question "Who would I be without me?" Through a powerful client story, Jim illustrates how ego identity quietly runs our lives and why releasing it doesn't leave you emptyвҖ”it makes you present.В  (12:04) Awareness is the light that dissolves ego Why your ego cannot survive scrutiny and observation, and how shifting from ego to soul as your primary vantage point changes what's possible for you.В  (18:03) The soul as parent, the ego as child A vivid metaphor for understanding the soul's role as observerвҖ”watching the ego's drama without being consumed by it and gently calling you toward expansion.В  (24:30) You don't need a birthdayвҖ”you have an incarnation date Why recognizing that you've never truly died shifts your operating frequency from ego fear to soulful power, and what it really means to become more spiritual.В  Listen, apply, and enjoy!В  Transformational TakeawayВ  You don't need to destroy your ego. You need to stop believing that it is you. Every fear, every limitation, every "I'm not good enough" belongs to the egoвҖ”a learned identity formed in childhood that no longer serves you. You are the soul observing the ego, present and aware, knowing everything is going to be fine. You have an incarnation date, not a birth date. You have never truly died. Stop fighting the ego. Simply become aware. Awareness is the light. Flip the switch.В  Mentioned Resources: https://www.jimfortin.com/soul Part 1 LetвҖҷs Connect:В  Instagram | Facebook | YouTube | LinkedInВ  LIKED THE EPISODE?В  If you're the kind of person who likes to help others, then share this with your friends and family. If you have found value, they will too. Please leave a review on Apple Podcasts so we can reach more people.В  Listening on Spotify? Please leave a comment below. We would love to hear from you!В  With gratitude, Jim

title: 7 Steps to Surviving Any Breakup
summary: She called me a broken bird. And she was right. I was giving a woman a beauty treatment when I could just feel her sadness. She didn't tell me she'd been cheated on. She didn't tell me she'd been mourning for months. But I knew. And the one thing I said to her changed everything. In this episode, I'm sharing the 7 things you need to do immediately after a breakup вҖ” whether you got dumped, caught them cheating, or just know in your gut it's over. This isn't fluff. This is what actually works. title: Leadership & Valor: A Conversation with Vice Admiral James Crawford
summary: What does real leadership look like when the building is on fireвҖҰliterally? In this episode, IвҖҷm joined by my good friend and guest host Michael Savage and a man whose life defines servant leadership at the highest level, Vice Admiral James W. Crawford III. Jim was inside the Pentagon on 9/11 when it was struck. He later helped advise on some of the most consequential military decisions of our generation. And today, he serves as President of Texas Southern University, shaping the next generation of leaders. This conversation is not about flashy leadership. ItвҖҷs not about titles, money, or Instagram fame. ItвҖҷs about character. ItвҖҷs about humility. ItвҖҷs about what you draw on when all eyes turn to you and the pressure is on. Jim said something that stopped me in my tracks. In times of stress, you either become who you are or you revert to your training. That day in the Pentagon changed the trajectory of his life. And yet, when he talks about it, you hear gratitude, not ego. Service, not self. We went deep into what leadership really demands. Humility as a shield against ego. Authenticity in unguarded moments. Mission first. People always. Jim opened up about imposter syndrome, about looking in the mirror at three stars on his uniform and still being astounded it was him. He shared how working on his grandfatherвҖҷs tobacco farm shaped his values, and why he chose education over seven figure corporate roles after retiring from the Navy. His answer was simple and powerful. Service does not end when the uniform comes off. If you are an entrepreneur, a founder, a CEO, a parent, or someone who simply wants to lead your life better, this is a masterclass. We talked about raising agile thinkers in an AI driven world. About how fear can freeze you if you let it. About why the best leaders are not the smartest person in the room, but the one who knows where their reservoir of strength comes from when the storm hits. JimвҖҷs reservoir is his faith. Yours might be something else. But you better know what it is. This episode reminded me why I started this show in the first place. Real leadership is about people and for people. And when you get that right, everything changes. Key Takeaways:    Why humility is the leaderвҖҷs greatest shield against ego and arrogance    What it means to be authentic in unguarded moments    Mission first. People always. How to integrate both without burning out    How to lead through crisis by reverting to your training and values    Why constant learning and agility are essential in an AI driven world    The importance of identifying your personal reservoir of strength before the storm comes    How service can and should continue long after titles and uniforms are gone Share this with someone who wants to lead better, serve deeper, and live with greater purpose. LetвҖҷs Max Out. Also donвҖҷt miss out on MAXOUT2026: Once a year, I open my home for an intimate one-day experience unlike anything else I do. This year, IвҖҷm making it even smaller, just 15 to 18 people. Together, weвҖҷll dive deep into the exact strategies I use to plan, visualize, and design the best year of my life and yours. title: 4 Nervous System Resets to Calm Your Mind & Body: Two to Release, Two to Restore
podcast_name: Alive & Well вҖ“ Burnout, Anxiety, Nervous System, Productivity, Time Management, Somatic Tools, Work-Life Balance
summary: It's 10pm. You finally lie down. The kids are in bed. Work is technically done.В  But your brain is still in twelve tabs. Your jaw is still clenched.В  You lie there trying to breathe slowly while your body hums with some leftover energy you cannot locate or name. Sound familiar? Here's the thing nobody tells you: calming down doesn't work when your nervous system is still activated. You're not broken. You're just doing it in the wrong order. This episode is the fix.В  I break down two practices to release and discharge what your body has been storing all day, and two to gently guide your nervous system back into safety and rest. By the end, you'll understand what's actually happening in your body when you skip the discharge step, why the order matters, and how this sequence can help you settle вҖ” and sleep вҖ” more naturally.В  Bonus: this works awesome for your kids at bedtime too. What You'll Learn: Why 'trying to calm down' often backfires вҖ” and what your body actually needs firstThe neuroscience behind why fight-or-flight is a mobilizing response (your body wants to move)The full 4-step reset sequence and how to use it todayGrab the Nervous System Reset Guide (free)

title: Your Body Is Speaking: Cyclical Wisdom, Fertility Awareness & Reclaiming Trust with Emma Goff
podcast_name: Healing From Abuse & Trauma - Victim 2 Victor
summary: What if your menstrual cycle wasnвҖҷt something to manage or fix - but a language to learn? In this episode, IвҖҷm joined by Emma Goff, fertility awareness educator, miscarriage and bereavement doula, certified SymptoPro instructor, and founder of Emmamma, a body literacy and cyclical wellness education platform. As Your Cyclical Doula, Emma supports women in understanding their bodiesвҖҷ natural rhythms from menarche through menopause, helping them decode fertility signals, hormonal patterns, and emotional shifts with curiosity and compassion. We explore how learning to chart and interpret the bodyвҖҷs signs can become a powerful pathway to self-trust, emotional healing, and embodied empowerment. Emma shares how her work with fertility, miscarriage, postpartum experiences, and reproductive health has deepened her understanding of grief, resilience, and the nervous systemвҖҷs role in cyclical wellbeing. This conversation gently challenges the cultural narratives that frame womenвҖҷs bodies as unpredictable or broken - and offers a new lens: one where the body is wise, responsive, and worthy of being listened to. In this episode, we discuss: вҖў Body literacy as a foundation for empowerment вҖў How cycles reflect emotional and nervous system patterns вҖў The impact of reproductive loss and how to support healing вҖў Rebuilding trust after medical dismissal or trauma вҖў Cyclical living as a mindset shift, not a rulebook вҖў Using fertility awareness beyond pregnancy prevention вҖў Simple ways to begin reconnecting with your bodyвҖҷs wisdom If youвҖҷve ever felt disconnected from your body, confused by your cycle, or curious about what it means to live in rhythm with yourself, this episode is for you. рҹҺ§ Learn more about Emma Goff & Emmamma: https://emmamma.com/ https://www.instagram.com/mrsemmagoff/?hl=en Send a text Support the show Connect with me:   Take the diagnostic tool kit to stop repeating patterns in your relationships: https://victim2victor.net/the-chemistry-trap/  Book a call with me: http://calendly.com/v2vhealingjourney  Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/victim2victor_anuverma/  Linkedin: https://www.linkedin.com/in/anuvermav2v  Access all resources: https://linktr.ee/healing.journeyy

title: Want to protect your money? Diversify your investments
summary: When it comes to investing money, don't put all your eggs in one basket. Spread out your investments among different types of assets and sectors, so you're not overexposed if one of them takes a hit. In this episode, we'll walk you through different types of assets, how your investment strategy should change depending on your age and needs, and a simple rule of thumb to calculate your stock versus bond allocation. title: Bridging the Political Divide with Roy Cooper
podcast_name: Laugh Lines with Kim & Penn Holderness
summary: This week on Laugh Lines, Penn and I sit down with former North Carolina Governor Roy Cooper to talk about bridging divides in a world that feels angrier by the minute. And yes, we know - politics can feel exhausting. But this conversation felt different. It felt human. It felt hopeful. It made us smile. Governor Cooper shares why he believes we are not as divided as social media makes us think, what it takes to work across party lines, and how faith has shaped his life of public service. We also talk about Penn's Dad, UNC basketball, and a reminder that community happens in real life, not just in the comment section. If youвҖҷve been feeling weary about the state of things, we hope this conversation gives you a breath of fresh air. True leadership stands in someone elseвҖҷs shoes, values decency, and doesn't try to divide. Thanks for being here with us. We love to hear from you! title: How To Train Your Mind: The surprising benefits of play
podcast_name: Growth Mindset Psychology: The Science of Self-Improvement
summary: Turns out you don't learn faster when you're stressed, you learn faster when you're happy! So let's talk about treating your brain like a friend, not a factory worker. Muscles respond to stress with pretty linear upgrades; brains donвҖҷt. Brains upgrade through meaning, novelty, and attentionвҖ”especially when something feels like play. ThatвҖҷs why memory champions donвҖҷt вҖңgrindвҖқ digits: they build absurd stories and mental rooms and suddenly their recall looks like magic. Same with skills.  Stress is usefulвҖ”but mostly as a signal: вҖңavoid that.вҖқ If you want real output, your job is to become a betterВ teacherВ to yourself: make the hard thing interesting enough that youвҖҷll come back tomorrow.    Turn one task into a game (score it, time it, level it up).    Find the вҖңfun entry pointвҖқ and start there, not with drills.    Reduce stress friction: smaller reps, better cues, better environment. Listen inвҖ”and pick one thing to make genuinely enjoyable today. title: Episode 216: ШҙШҙ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶЩҮ Ъ©Щ… ШҙШҜЩҶ Ш§Ш№ШӘЩ…Ш§ШҜ Ш¬Щ…Ш№ЫҢ ЩҲ ШұШ§ЩҮЪ©Ш§ШұЩҮШ§Шҙ
summary: ЪҶШұШ§ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШұЩҲШІЩҮШ§ ШІЩҲШҜ ЩҮЩ…ШҜЫҢЪҜЩҮ ШұЩҲ ЩӮШ¶Ш§ЩҲШӘ Щ…ЫҢЪ©ЩҶЫҢЩ…Шҹ ШӘЩҲ Ш§ЩҫЫҢШІЩҲШҜ ЫІЫұЫ¶ Ш§ШІ ЩҫШ§ШҜЪ©ШіШӘ В«Щ„Ш§Щ… ШӘШ§ Ъ©Щ„Ш§Щ…В» ШҜШұШЁШ§ШұЩҮ Щ…ЩҲШ¶ЩҲШ№ЫҢ ШӯШұЩҒ Щ…ЫҢШІЩҶЩ… Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁЫҢ ШіШұ ЩҲ ШөШҜШ§ ШҜШ§ШұЩҮ ШұЩҲШ§ШЁШ· ЩҲ ШҜЩҲШіШӘЫҢ ЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…Ш§ ШұЩҲ ЩҶШ§ШЁЩҲШҜ Щ…ЫҢЪ©ЩҶЩҮ: Ъ©Щ… ШҙШҜЩҶ Ш§Ш№ШӘЩ…Ш§ШҜ Ш¬Щ…Ш№ЫҢ ЩҲ ШӘШ§Ш«ЫҢШұШҙ ШұЩҲЫҢ ШұЩҲШ§ЩҶ Щ…Ш§. Щ…Ш§ ШіШ§Щ„ ЩҮШ§ШіШӘ Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁЩҮ Ш®Ш§Ш·Шұ Ш®ШЁШұЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШЁШҜШҢ ЩҶШ§Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢ ЩҲ Ш§ШЁЩҮШ§Щ…ШҢ ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ШӘ ШўЩ…Ш§ШҜЩҮ ШЁШ§Шҙ ЩҮШіШӘЫҢЩ… ЩҲ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШЁШ§Ш№Ш« ШҙШҜЩҮ ШіЫҢШіШӘЩ… Ш№ШөШЁЫҢ Щ…Ш§ ЩҒШұШіЩҲШҜЩҮ ШЁШҙЩҮ. ШӘЩҲ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§ЩҫЫҢШІЩҲШҜЫ¶ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶЩҮ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШҙЩҶШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢ Ъ©Щ… ШҙШҜЩҶ Ш§Ш№ШӘЩ…Ш§ШҜ Ш¬Щ…Ш№ЫҢ ШұЩҲ Щ…ЫҢШЁЫҢЩҶЫҢЩ… ЩҲ ЪҶЩҮШ§Шұ ШұШ§ЩҮЪ©Ш§Шұ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Щ…ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ЩҮ Ш§ЩҲЩҶ. Щ…ЩҶ ШЁШұШ§ШӘ ШӘЩҲШ¶ЫҢШӯ Щ…ЫҢШҜЩ… Ъ©ЩҮ ЪҶШұШ§ ЩҲЩӮШӘЫҢ Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘ Ъ©Щ… Щ…ЫҢШҙЩҮШҢ Щ…ШәШІ Щ…Ш§ ШЁЩҮ Ш¬Ш§ЫҢ ШўШұШ§Щ…ШҙШҢ ЩҒЩӮШ· ШӘЩҮШҜЫҢШҜЩҮШ§ ШұЩҲ Ш§ШіЪ©ЩҶ Щ…ЫҢЪ©ЩҶЩҮ ЩҲ ЪҶШ·ЩҲШұ ШІШ®Щ… ЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩӮШҜЫҢЩ…ЫҢ ШЁШ§Ш№Ш« Щ…ЫҢШҙЩҶ ШЁЩҮ ШўШҜЩ… ЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШұЩҲШЁШұЩҲЩ…ЩҲЩҶ ЩҲШ§Ъ©ЩҶШҙ ЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШӘЩҶШҜ ЩҶШҙЩҲЩҶ ШЁШҜЫҢЩ…. Ш§ЪҜЩҮ ШӘЩҲ ЩҮЩ… ШӯШі Щ…ЫҢЪ©ЩҶЫҢ Ш§Ш·ШұШ§ЩҒШӘ ЩҫШұ Ш§ШІ ШӘЫҢЪ©ЩҮ ЩҲ Ъ©ЩҶШ§ЫҢЩҮ ШҙШҜЩҮ ЫҢШ§ ШҜЫҢЪҜЩҮ Щ…Ш«Щ„ ЩӮШЁЩ„ ЩҶЩ…ЫҢШӘЩҲЩҶЫҢ ШұШ§ШӯШӘ ШӯШұЩҒ ШҜЩ„ШӘ ШұЩҲ ШЁШІЩҶЫҢШҢ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЪҜЩҒШӘЪҜЩҲ ШЁЩҮШӘ Ъ©Щ…Ъ© Щ…ЫҢЪ©ЩҶЩҮ ШұЫҢШҙЩҮ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШЁЫҢ ШӘЩҒШ§ЩҲШӘЫҢ ЩҲ ЩҒШ§ШөЩ„ЩҮ ЩҮШ§ ШұЩҲ ЩҫЫҢШҜШ§ Ъ©ЩҶЫҢ. ЩҶШ§ЪҜЩҒШӘЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЪҶШ§Щ„Шҙ ЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШұЩҲШІШ§ЩҶЩҮ Щ…Ш§ ШҜШұ ШұЩҲШ§ШЁШ·В  ЩҲ Ш§ШӘЩҒШ§ЩӮШ§ШӘ ШІЩҶШҜЪҜЫҢВ  ШЁШ§В  ШІШЁШ§ЩҶ ШіШ§ШҜЩҮ ЩҲ Ш№Ш§Щ…ЫҢШ§ЩҶЩҮ ШӘЩҲШіШ· ШіЩҲЩ„Щ…Ш§ШІ ШЁШұЩӮЪҜЫҢШұШҢ Щ„Ш§ЫҢЩҒ Ъ©ЩҲЪҶ ЩҲ Ш§ШіШӘШұШ§ШӘЪҳЫҢШіШӘ ШұЩҲШ§ШЁШ· Щ…ЩҲШ«ШұШҢ ШұЩҲШ§ЩҶ ШҜШұЩ…Ш§ЩҶЪҜШұ ШҜШұ ШӘЩҲШұЩҶШӘЩҲ Ъ©Ш§ЩҶШ§ШҜШ§ Ъ©ЩҲЪҶЫҢЩҶЪҜ ШЁШ§ ШіЩҲЩ„Щ…Ш§ШІ Episode 215: ШӘЪ©ЩҶЫҢЪ© ЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ЩҮ ШЁШ§ Ш°ЩҮЩҶ Ш®ШіШӘЩҮ ЩҲ Ш§ЩҒЪ©Ш§Шұ ШӘЪ©ШұШ§ШұЫҢEpisode 139: ШіЩҲЪҜЫҢШұЫҢ ЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШҙЩҶШ§Ш®ШӘЫҢШіЩҒШ§ШұШҙ Ъ©ШӘШ§ШЁ "ЩҶЩӮШҙ ШҜШұЩҲЩҶ" ЩҲШұЪ©ШҙШ§Щҫ вҖңШӘЪ©ЩҶЫҢЪ© ЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ШұШІЪҜШ°Ш§ШұЫҢвҖқВ  ШҜЩҲШұЩҮвҖҢ В«Щ…Ш№Ш¬ШІЩҮвҖҢ ЩӮЩ„Щ… ЩҲ Ъ©Ш§ШәШ° ШЁШ§ Щ…ШҜЫҢШӘЫҢШҙЩҶ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШұЩҮШ§ЫҢЫҢ Ш§ШІ Ш§ШіШ§ШұШӘ ШӘШұШіВ» ШҜШұЫҢШ§ЩҒШӘ ШҜЩҲШұЩҮ "ШұЩҮШ§ЫҢЫҢ Ш§ШІ ЩҮЫҢЩҲЩ„Ш§ЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘШұШі ЩҲ ЩҶЪҜШұШ§ЩҶЫҢ" ШҜШұЫҢШ§ЩҒШӘ ШҜЩҲШұЩҮ Ш®ЩҲШҜ Щ…ШӯЪ©Щ… ШҜШұ ЩҮЩҒШӘ ЩӮШҜЩ… ШҜШұЫҢШ§ЩҒШӘ ШҜЩҲШұЩҮ ЩҮЩҒШӘ ШіЩҶЪҜ ШЁЩҶШ§ЫҢ ШұШ§ШЁШ·ЩҮ Щ…ЩҲШ«Шұ Lam Ta Kalam is a Well-received Farsi Podcast Episode 175: How to Manage Time? title: Episode 136 - The Light Doctor with Dr. Martin Moore-Ede
summary: What if one of the biggest drivers of your productivity wasn't your calendar, your habits, or your willpower but the light around you?В  In this episode of Productivity Smarts, host Gerald J. Leonard sits down with Dr. Martin Moore-Ede, a world-leading expert in circadian biology and founder of the Circadian Light Research Center. Dr. Moore-Ede, known as "The Light Doctor," reveals a hidden productivity lever most of us overlook entirely: the light around us.  We live in a world of artificial light, but not all light is created equal. Dr. Moore-Ede explains how modern LED and fluorescent lighting disrupt our internal biological clocks, leading to poor sleep, foggy thinking, weakened immune systems, and even long-term health risks. What begins as a conversation about light evolves into a fundamental discussion on human biology, explaining how the blue spectrum in everyday bulbs tells our brain it is daytime, halting vital repair processes, suppressing cancer-fighting melatonin, and throwing our entire system out of sync.  Gerald and Dr. Moore-Ede explore practical, science-backed strategies to harness light for peak performance. Learn the four key pillars of a "healthy light diet," why morning outdoor time is non-negotiable, and how to choose evening lighting that will not sabotage your sleep. Discover how companies worldwide are using these principles to boost workforce productivity by over 30 percent, reduce errors, and improve safety.  This episode shows how to work with your biology rather than against it. If you want to sharpen your focus, enhance your energy, sleep deeply, and unlock a new level of sustainable productivity, it starts with the light you see. title: 347  |  Guided Mindful Decluttering Session - Declutter Your Bedroom Part 1
podcast_name: Declutter Your Chaos - Minimalism, Decluttering, Home Organization
summary: Hey Guys,  In this episode I explain how to prepare your time, zones, supplies and energy for decluttering your space.  If you want deeper support, weekly guidance, and accountability, you can learn more about theВ Year-Long Program by clicking the link in the show notes.   Episode Overview This episode kicks off Bedroom Week on Declutter Your Chaos. Throughout the week, you'll be guided through mindful decluttering sessions for the primary bedroom, with a clear plan so you're not guessing where to start or how to prepare.  Before we declutter anything, today's episode focuses on preparation. You'll learn exactly how to set yourself up for success so the guided sessions on Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday feel doable, contained, and effective.  The mindfulness theme for February is self-worth. If you haven't listened to Episode 346, start thereвҖ”it explores how self-worth impacts buying habits and how mindfulness helps interrupt that cycle.   Today's Focus: How to Prep for Bedroom Decluttering You'll walk through four non-negotiable prep steps that make the actual decluttering sessions work.  1. Choose Your Time These are full decluttering sessions, not quick resets.  Guidelines:    Block 2вҖ“3 hours per session    Plan as many sessions as you need for the space.    Schedule them within a two-week window    Consider:    When your energy is highest (morning vs. afternoon)    Likely interruptions (kids, partners, responsibilities)    Not spacing sessions too far apartвҖ”dragging it out leads to frustration and burnout    These sessions have a beginning and an end. Think of them like a short race, not a lifestyle you have to maintain forever.   2. Choose Your Zones Break the bedroom into clear, contained sections so each session has a defined focus.  This week's zones:    Tuesday: Floor (including bins or random items on the floor)    Wednesday: Surfaces (dressers, nightstands, vanities)    Thursday: Inside furniture (drawers, cabinets, storage inside the bedroom)    You can adjust zones to fit your spaceвҖ”left/right sides, under the bed, specific furnitureвҖ”but each zone should reasonably fit into a 2вҖ“3 hour session.   3. Choose Your Supplies You don't need much.  Required:    Post-its    A marker or Sharpie    Optional:    Bags, boxes, bins, or pilesвҖ”whatever helps you move quickly    Post-its are essential so you can label categories clearly and avoid decision fatigue while you're decluttering.   4. Choose Your Energy This is about physical, mental, and emotional readiness.    Get enough sleep    Start when you're reasonably energized    Shift how you're relating to the space    Approach this with curiosity instead of pressure. Treat it as an experiment:    What might you learn about yourself?    What space might open up?    What could become possible once things are cleared?    The items you're letting go of belong to the past. The work you're doing is happening in the presentвҖ”to create room for what comes next.   Tomorrow, you'll be guided step-by-step throughВ Zone One.  If this episode would help someone you know, share it with them.  See you tomorrow.  Your Decluttering Year Program:В If you would like more information about my year long decluttering program clickВ HERE.  If this episode helped you, please leave a review or share it with someone who needs it.   Download my free decluttering planner here:В https://declutteryourchaos.com/decluttering-planner  Let's connect:  рҹ“ё Instagram:В @declutteryourchaos   рҹҢҗ Website: www.declutteryourchaos.com  рҹӣ‘ Disclaimer:  I am not a licensed therapist, counselor, or mental health professional. This podcast is for informational and entertainment purposes only and is not intended as a substitute for professional advice, diagnosis, or treatment. If you are experiencing emotional distress or mental health concerns, please seek help from a qualified mental health provider for more helpful tips and updates.  Sponsored Content Disclosure:  Some episodes include references to products I genuinely use and love.  When I partner with a brand, I may receive compensation for sharing it here.  I only talk about products I've personally chosen to bring into my own home and life, and I never recommend anything I wouldn't use myself.

title: EP 629: Grief, Secure Relationships, and Healing
podcast_name: LetвҖҷs Get Vulnerable: Relationship and Dating Advice
summary: This episode is a deeply personal one. IвҖҷm sharing from a place of real-time grief after an unexpected loss in my family, and IвҖҷm letting you see what it looks like to move through heartbreak as someone who has done the work to become securely attached. WeвҖҷre talking about grief, emotional safety, and what truly defines a secure relationship, not perfection, but repair. If youвҖҷve ever numbed your pain, pushed love away when you needed it most, or believed that being вҖңstrongвҖқ meant not feelingвҖҰ this episode is for you. Inside this episode: Why grief hits differently when youвҖҷre securely attached - the difference between numbing your pain (like I did for years) and allowing yourself to feel it in a regulated, supported way.The truth about secure relationships - how I caught my old disorganized attachment patterns coming up in my marriage, and why secure love isnвҖҷt about never having rupturesвҖҰ itвҖҷs about repair.How to stop running from your emotions and create emotional safety within yourself - because the depth at which you allow yourself to feel grief is also the depth at which you get to experience love, joy, and connection. Grief is not linear. There is no вҖңright wayвҖқ to do it. But I want you to hear this: if you are avoiding your emotions, numbing out, or telling yourself youвҖҷre better off alone, thatвҖҷs not strength. ThatвҖҷs a sign your nervous system doesnвҖҷt feel safe enough yet. And healing is possible. The only way forward is through. And you donвҖҷt have to do it alone. If youвҖҷre ready to stop running from your emotions, rewire your attachment patterns, and embody secure attachment so you can have the kind of relationship where you are seen, heard, and loved, even on your worst days,I invite you to apply for my Private 1:1 Coaching container, Empowered Secure Love. We only have a few spots available due to the intensive nature of this work. Apply here: рҹ‘ү https://www.drmorgancoaching.co/esl-breakthrough рҹ”— Additional Episode Links:Take the Free Attachment Style Quiz вҶ’ https://drmorgancoaching.com/quizFollow me on IG вҶ’ @drmorgancoachingGrab my book:   Grab my best selling book Love Magnet

title: Unapologetic Truth: The Relentless Spirit of Adam вҖңPacmanвҖқ Jones
summary: From electrifying NFL fields to rewriting his own narrative with resilience, wisdom, and unapologetic truth, Adam "Pacman" Jones is more than an athleteвҖ”he's a force of nature. A dynamic leader who inspires redemption, responsibility, and real talk, Pacman brings his signature "go get it" mentality to every aspect of his life, proving that with unwavering dedication and a commitment to authenticity, you can redefine your legacy and empower those around you. Get ready to be inspired by a man who lives by the mantra: "work hard, play hard" and whose impact extends far beyond the gridiron. Takeaways: The Power of Unrelenting Work Ethic: Pacman highlights that while talent is important, an unmatched work ethic is what truly separates good from great, emphasizing that relentless effort can overcome natural ability. Strategic Preparation as a Skillset: Coming from a background where he always played with older, more experienced individuals, Pacman developed an acute awareness of strategic preparation, translating his multifaceted athletic background into a unique mental edge that propelled him to success. The Evolution of a Leader: From needing a supportive circle in his early career, Pacman transformed into a pillar of support for others, understanding the profound impact of intentional mentorship and building genuine relationships that transcend the paycheck. Mentorship Through Vulnerability: Recognizing the value of his own experiences, Pacman passionately advocates for showing vulnerability and honesty, especially with children, using his past struggles and triumphs to teach invaluable life lessons and foster growth. Intentional Community Impact: Beyond the public eye, Pacman is deeply committed to supporting his community and youth, choosing to act with intention and genuine care rather than seeking recognition, focusing on tangible actions that empower individuals to overcome significant challenges. Sound Bytes: "What separates good and great is work ethic. Period point one." "That's my calling, man. That's my calling. Like, I was one of those kids who lost his dad early, stayed with my grandma." "I truly believe in you are what you hang around." title: Declutter to Fit the Life You Want (Tough Love with Peter Walsh) | Clutterbug Podcast #314
podcast_name: ClutterBug - Organize, Clean and Transform your Home & Life
summary: Decluttering, home organization, and clutter psychologyвҖ”organizing expert (and MY HERO!) Peter Walsh (Clean Sweep, Space Invaders) joins me to talk about why clutter isnвҖҷt about the stuff and how overconsumption keeps our homes overwhelmed. If youвҖҷre stuck in perfectionism, struggling with the mental load, or feeling guilt about waste and donations, this episode will help you declutter with clarity and confidence. AND I tried to only fan girl a little bit!Peter shares what heвҖҷs learned after 20+ years of helping people and opening over 10,000 boxes of clutter on Space Invaders, including why real change comes from addressing the story behind the stuffвҖ”not buying better organizing products.We discuss:   The link between clutter andВ consumption/coveting   Why decluttering isnвҖҷt an eventвҖ”itвҖҷs aВ mindset and process   The key question:В What do I want from my life, and what do I want from this room?   Practical starting points (5 items a day, trash bag tango, reverse coat hanger trick)   Storage solutions vs the reality ofВ space limits   How to avoid rushing people through change (intention vs impact) You can find more Clutterbug content here: Main YouTube Channel: @Clutterbug Website:В http://www.clutterbug.me TikTok:В https://www.tiktok.com/@clutterbug_me Instagram:В https://www.instagram.com/clutterbug_me/ Facebook:В https://www.facebook.com/Clutterbug.Me/ Peter's Website: https://peterwalshdesign.com/ #Clutterbug #PeterWalsh #Decluttering #HomeOrganization #OrganizingTips #ClutterFree #DeclutterYourHome #ClutterPsychology #Minimalism #Overconsumption #NoBuy #LowBuy #ShoppingAddiction #MentalLoad #Overwhelmed #Perfectionism #CleaningMotivation #SpaceInvaders #CleanSweep #OrganizedHome #SimpleLiving #MindsetShift #HomeDeclutter #ReduceStress #StopBuying #Consumerism #FamilyOrganization #ADHDOrganization #StorageSolutions #HoardingHelp Learn more about your ad choices. title: Walk With Weight: Michael Easter On The Evolutionary Case For Rucking, Building Real Resilience & How To Stay Adventure-Ready For Life
summary: Michael Easter is a New York Times bestselling author, UNLV professor, and the mind behind вҖңWalk With Weight.вҖқ This conversation explores rucking, the evolutionary movement pattern humans are built for that modern fitness has largely overlooked. We discuss why it affects body composition differently, how GPS navigation impacts cognition, and why optimization culture can undermine resilience. Plus, he challenges my assumptions about comfort zones. Michael's insights are practical, contrarian, and rooted in science and experience. Enjoy! title: Amanda Knox: How I Reclaimed My Freedom After Wrongful Conviction of Murder | Mindset | E28
podcast_name: Miracle Mentality with Tim Storey (Motivation, Self Help, and Mental Health)
summary: While studying abroad in Italy, somebody broke into Amanda KnoxвҖҷs home in the middle of the night, where they brutally assaulted and murdered her roommate. Despite her innocence, Amanda was quickly named the number one suspect and was subject to nearly four years in prison for a crime she didnвҖҷt commit. While she was released from prison nearly 15 years ago, it took her several years to reclaim her sense of freedom. In this episode of Miracle Mentality, Amanda walks through her journey of wrongful conviction and how she uses her experience to help others develop their purpose.В  Amanda Knox is an exoneree, journalist, public speaker, bestselling author, and podcast host. She serves on the advisory council for the Frederick Douglass Project for Justice, is an Innocence Network Ambassador, and sits on the board of directors for The Innocence Center.В  In this episode, Tim and Amanda discuss: (00:00) Introduction (02:24) AmandaвҖҷs Definition of Freedom (06:33) Finding Meaning in Wrongful ConvictionВ  (11:06) From Studying Abroad to a Prison Cell (18:07) Daily Life While Isolated in PrisonВ  (23:35) The True Power of Prayer (28:09) Letting Go of Expectations for Your Life (31:53) AmandaвҖҷs Purpose and Mission Post-Prison (39:46) Collaborating with Nikki Glaser (43:58) What It Means to Lead With Love Amanda Knox is an exoneree, journalist, public speaker, author of the New York Times bestselling memoir, Waiting to Be Heard, and host of the podcast Hard Knox. Between 2007 and 2015, she spent nearly four years in prison and eight years on trial for a murder she didnвҖҷt commit, and became a notorious tabloid story in the process. She sits on the advisory council for the Frederick Douglass Project for Justice, serves as an Innocence Network Ambassador, and is on The Innocence Center Board of Directors. title: Gary Zukav's Personal Experiences - Linda Part 3
summary: Discover a new way of seeing and experiencing the world. Start your journey to multisensory perception HERE: https://seatofthesoul.com/mutli-five-sensory/ In this deeply intimate continuation of his journey, Gary Zukav reveals what happens when the boundaries between physical and non-physical reality begin to dissolve. Through his ongoing Spiritual Partnership with Linda Francis after her Soul's transition, Gary guides listeners through the paradox of profound presence and profound loss existing simultaneously. He shares the bewildering terrain of speaking with Linda daily while also aching for her physical touch - the five-sensory longing to hear her laugh. This is not a story of "getting over" grief; it is a testament to expanding beyond the personalityвҖҷs limitations to discover a connection that transcends the Earth School's physical constraints. The confusion Gary describes is not weakness - it is the natural friction that occurs when frightened parts of the personality demand the world remain as it was, while loving parts recognize a deeper intimacy emerging. His teacher's instruction seemed impossible: forget about missing Linda and enjoy life. Yet this paradox held the key to Authentic Power. The pain of the personality can coexist with the reality of the SoulвҖҷs presence, and the intellect - designed only for five-sensory perception - cannot reconcile what the heart already knows. The solution Gary discovered was a Responsible Choice: to experience greater intimacy with Linda, he had to stop clinging to the old forms of their relationship. By setting the intention to relax into joy, he created space for a multisensory connection to emerge - one where Linda appears in morning light, in clouds, and in moments of presence so powerful they fill every cell of his being. This is the promise of multisensory perception: not the loss of those we love, but an expansion into experiencing them in ways the five senses alone could never access. What YouвҖҷll Discover in This Episode:    (00:02) вҖ” The Vast Scope of Human Emotion: Gary explores the full range of emotional experienceвҖ”from rage and victimhood to gratitude and aweвҖ”and why you are far more than any single snapshot of feeling.    (04:15) вҖ” Choosing Between Love and Fear: How the new consciousness gives you the unprecedented ability to distinguish frightened parts of your personality from loving parts, and to make the Responsible Choice of which to act on.    (06:50) вҖ” "Linda Is With You": The first words Gary's teacher spoke after Linda's Soul's transition, and why frightened parts of the personality demand the world remain unchanged while love opens to new possibilities.    (11:03) вҖ” Forgetting About Missing Her: The paradoxical instruction to "forget about missing Linda and enjoy life"вҖ”and how setting this intention opened the door to experiencing her multisensory presence more fully.    (14:34) вҖ” Seeing Linda in Everything: Gary describes the first time he saw Linda in the morning light through trees, in clouds, and felt her presence so powerfully it filled his entire being.    (18:14) вҖ” Beyond the Intellect's Limits: Why the intellect was never designed to understand non-physical reality, and how the new consciousness allows you to become what you are rather than just describe it.

title: 288: Dynamics of Vitality VI: Friendship & Justice
summary: #288: In this capstone episode, Sharif and Kevin explore a powerful question: Do you have to fix yourself before you can serve others? As they unpack the virtue of justice, Kevin challenges the common belief that relief from anxiety, depression, or anger must come first. Instead, he argues that vitality begins when attention shifts outwardвҖ”toward real duties, real people, and real bonds. But they donвҖҷt stop there. Drawing from AristotleвҖҷs Nicomachean Ethics, Kevin makes a bold claim: friendship goes beyond justice. While justice orients us toward the good of others, itвҖҷs genuine friendshipвҖ”shared meaning, shared effort, shared attentionвҖ”that becomes an inexhaustible source of enthusiasm in work and life. From teachers losing early excitement to professionals searching for lasting motivation, Sharif and Kevin show why projects and pleasures eventually fadeвҖ”while bonds with people never do. Find more at https://OptimalWork.com

title: How Psychedelic Experiences Support Growth When the Nervous System Is Prepared and Integrated
summary: Psychedelics are having a cultural moment. Research is promising. Stories of healing are everywhere. But here's the truth: these experiences aren't magic cures. And they aren't right for every nervous system at every time.  В   In this episode, Elisabeth Kristof and Jennifer Wallace slow the conversation down. Instead of asking, "Do psychedelics heal trauma?" They explore a more grounded question: What becomes possible when psychedelic or peak somatic experiences are approached through the lens of nervous system safety, preparation, and integration?  В   If you've been curious about psychedelics, already had experiences, or feel unsure whether they're right for you, this episode offers nuance, research, and deep nervous system perspective.  Because post-traumatic growth isn't about becoming someone new.  It's about becoming more available to the life that's already waiting for you.  В   Topic Covered    Why psychedelics may reorganize meaning, not just reduce symptoms    How trauma fragments narrative and how safety allows integration    The science of psychological flexibility and why it predicts long-term outcomes    What "somatic journeying" is and why it can feel disorienting    The importance of preparation, titration, and facilitator trust    Why intensity does not equal healing    Psychedelics vs antidepressants in research on connectedness    Default Mode Network (DMN), identity rigidity, and belief updating    Why creativity often emerges when survival softens    The risks of over-reliance and "chasing the medicine"    Why discernment and self-trust matter more than hype    В   ChaptersВ   00:00 вҖ“ Psychedelics Aren't Magic CuresвҖЁ  03:00 вҖ“ Meaning-Making & Narrative ReorganizationвҖЁ  08:58 вҖ“ Psychological Flexibility & Emotional CapacityвҖЁ  17:00 вҖ“ Preparation, Somatic Journeying & IntegrationвҖЁ  23:29 вҖ“ Connectedness & Relational RepairвҖЁ  34:33 вҖ“ Identity, Neuro Tags & the Default Mode NetworkвҖЁ  41:03 вҖ“ Creativity as a Byproduct of SafetyвҖЁ  48:14 вҖ“ Discernment, Industry Hype & Self-Trust  В   Calls to Action:    Neurosomatic Intelligence is now enrolling : https://neurosomaticintelligence.com/nsi-certification    Sacred Synapse: an educational YouTube channel founded by Jennifer Wallace that explores nervous system regulation, applied neuroscience, consciousness, and psychedelic preparation and integration through Neurosomatic Intelligence.В     В   Wayfinder Journal: Track nervous system patterns and support preparation and integration through Neurosomatic Intelligence. title: On Different Timelines: Embrace Your Journey
summary: Welcome to this weekвҖҷs episode with Anna OвҖҷConnor В .This week Anna talks honestly about grief coexisting with growth, the law of attraction, and the everyday efforts of self-development. В  She explores the idea that weвҖҷre all on different timelines and offers practical guidance вҖ” 10 ways to embrace your timeline вҖ” including using rejection as redirection, positioning yourself for what you want, aligning habits with desires, and acting like your future self. Anna also reflects on leveling up her podcast and business, encouraging listeners to keep chipping away and be kind to themselves through the ups and downs.

title: Unapologetically Provocative: UNCORKED with SARAH CHAN
summary: What happens when a 26-year-old refuses to sit down, shut up, or soften her edge for our comfort? Well, youвҖҷre about to find out. In this UNCORKED and unapologetically raw conversation, Tim Windsor sits down with Sarah Chan, a bold, brash entrepreneur building her own marketing company while calling out what she sees as cowardice in leadership, business, education, and politics. From turning a failing Saks Fifth Avenue counter into six-figure sales with zero experience to doubling her companyвҖҷs revenue in a year, Sarah shares how self-belief was not handed to her but forged through results, rejection, and relentless honesty. This episode explores the power of conviction, the cost of speaking the truth, and the difference between opponents and enemies in a polarized world. Together, Tim and Sarah dive into work-life balance, generational frustration, insecure leaders, broken systems, healthy debate, and why uncomfortable conversations might be the very thing that saves us from cultural collapse. You may not agree with everything said in this episode. Good. That is the point. This conversation is not about echo chambers. It is about wrestling with ideas, challenging bias, and rediscovering the courage to speak with integrity, even when it costs you. title: The Comeback with Sian Horn

title: Kat John, being. with Lael Stone | Coming Home to Yourself вҷҘпёҺ
summary: What if authenticity isnвҖҷt something you find... but something you return to? In this warm, honest, and at times deeply moving conversation, I sit down with Kat John; coach, author, and one of those rare humans who just radiates something you want to lean into. Kat helps people come home to themselves, their hearts, and what really matters. And in this conversation, she does exactly that - for herself, and for all of us listening. We explore: The ego voice that tells us weвҖҷre not doing enough - and how to hold it with compassion instead of feeding itThe dance between significance and simplicity - and why we donвҖҷt have to chooseKatвҖҷs remarkable healing journey - from brain surgery and chronic pain to living pain-free through the power of visualisationWhat it means to be cut off from your body early in life, and the slow, brave work of coming home to itFive Rhythms, movement, and why healing so often lives in the body rather than the mindThe complex, beautiful, challenging space of being a step-parentPerimenopause, rage, and learning to feel the feelings we were never allowed to haveWhat it looks like to build a brand and a life from a place of radical authenticity[Recorded October 2025] About Kat John: Kat John is a coach, speaker, and author of Authentic - a book about coming home to who you really are. Through her words, her work, and her deeply relatable presence, Kat reminds us that authenticity isnвҖҷt something to perform - itвҖҷs something to return to. title: Why Saying вҖңYesвҖқ Changed My Entire Life | Charlie Such
summary: The Tom's Talks Podcast is a place where we speak to a vast array of people from different walks of life and find out what makes them them. We will speak about their unique journey and delve into the highs and the lows which ultimately define them.В  In this powerful episode, we sit down with artist Charlie Such to talk about grief, purpose, mental health, and what it really takes to build something meaningful. After losing his mum to cancer eighteen months ago, CharlieвҖҷs perspective on life completely shifted. What started as a hobby became a mission. A need. A purpose. This conversation is honest, emotional, and packed with lessons around resilience, delayed gratification, and backing yourself вҖ” even when you donвҖҷt feel ready. title: Bill Gurley - Benchmark Partner, Backed Uber, Zillow, Stitch Fix | The Conveyor Belt That Ruins Your Life
podcast_name: Success Story with Scott D. Clary
summary: вһЎпёҸ Join 321,000 people who read my free weekly newsletter: https://newsletter.scottdclary.com вһЎпёҸ Like The Podcast? Leave A Rating: https://ratethispodcast.com/successstory Bill Gurley is a prominent American venture capitalist born in 1966 in Dickinson, Texas, who spent more than two decades as a general partner at Benchmark, the influential Silicon Valley firm he joined in 1999. Before moving into venture capital, he worked as a design engineer at Compaq and spent four years as a top-ranked Wall Street research analyst. At Benchmark, Gurley became known for backing transformative companies including Uber, GrubHub, OpenTable, Zillow, and Stitch Fix, with his early $12 million investment in Uber becoming one of the most celebrated bets in venture capital history. A sharp and often contrarian voice in Silicon Valley, he has long warned against unsustainable startup valuations and has been a vocal advocate for direct listings as an alternative to traditional IPOs. From Olympic sport to high-stakes business moments, the ability to deliberately shift your state can be the difference between choking and delivering. Nam unpacks why calm is a precursor to clarity, focus and execution вҖ” and shares simple, practical techniques to quickly reset your nervous system when you need to steady yourself fast. If youвҖҷve ever wanted to control your state instead of being controlled by it, this conversation shows you how. title: Episode 59: If Nothing Changes, Nothing Changes: Why You Stay Stuck
summary: In this solo episode, Belinda explores the powerful truth behind the phrase вҖңIf nothing changes, nothing changes. You say you want change, but find yourself repeating the same patterns, reactions, and choices. Belinda explains why change feels hard and not from a motivation or вҖңjust think positiveвҖқ lens, but from a nervous system and subconscious perspective. Because change isnвҖҷt just a mindset shift. ItвҖҷs a body shift. A pattern shift. An identity shift. YouвҖҷll learn how familiarity, survival mode, and subconscious programming keep you stuck in cycles,В  even when you truly want different. Belinda shares personal stories, real-life examples, and practical reflections to help you understand how safety, capacity, and small repeated actions retrain your system for growth. In this episode, we explore: Why the subconscious chooses familiar over healthyHow the nervous system resists unfamiliar changeThe role of identity in lasting transformationWhy change is a practice, not a decisionHow building capacity helps you tolerate discomfortThe link between regulation and perceptionSelf-reflection questions to interrupt old patternsA simple embodiment exercise to reconnect with yourself This episode is for anyone who feels stuck between wanting more and staying where it feels safe, and is ready to understand why. title: Everyday Habits That Build Real Self-Respect  | Dr Katherine Iscoe
summary: In this episode, we explore what self-respect actually means, beyond confidence, beyond success, beyond applause. For years, todayвҖҷs guest chased respect externally, having thoughts like: If you liked me, I liked me. If you approved, I felt safe. But as she shares, that way of living slowly disconnects you from yourself. IвҖҷm joined by Dr Katherine Iscoe to unpack: вҖў Why people-pleasing is often rooted in the вҖңfawnвҖқ trauma response вҖў How you might be subtly trading yourself away in everyday moments вҖў The restaurant вҖңsteakвҖқ example that reveals more than you think вҖў How to audit your calendar to see if you truly respect yourself вҖў Why boundaries arenвҖҷt just with other people, theyвҖҷre with yourself вҖў How overthinking steals years of your life вҖў The difference between chasing happiness and building self-respect вҖў What it actually looks like to make decisions from self-respect Self-respect isnвҖҷt loud.It isnвҖҷt aggressive.It isnвҖҷt about becoming selfish. ItвҖҷs about choosing yourself in small, daily ways, even when it feels uncomfortable. Because the truth is, confidence might get you in the room... but self-respect is what keeps you standing tall. If youвҖҷve ever felt like: * You say yes too often * You overthink simple decisions * You care more about othersвҖҷ opinions than your own * You donвҖҷt feel fully like yourself anymore This episode is for you. The life you want doesnвҖҷt start with more discipline. It starts with self-respect. title: Stop Living By Default: Debbie Millman on Designing a Life That Matters
summary: Debbie Millman is one of the worldвҖҷs most influential voiceson design, creativity, and personal growth. A renowned designer, author, educator, and host of the pioneering podcast Design Matters. In this powerful conversation, she explains why a meaningfullife doesnвҖҷt happen by chance itвҖҷs designed. From overcoming self-doubt to embracing failure, Debbie shares the mindset shifts that separate people who drift from those who build a life with purpose. We discuss: How to design a life with intention The real role of failure in long-term successScarcity vs. abundance thinking (and why itmatters)Why вҖңbusyвҖқ is a decisionNavigating self-doubt and imposter syndrome

title: Monday Morning with Myself | Choosing Courage Over Comfort
summary: This week, the quote on my planner is a BrenГ© Brown one of choosing courage over comfort. Outside of the comfort zone is the stretch zone. Courage means to be scared and do it anyway. and that's where you have to step into courage.

title: Why Change Feels Hard (Even When You Really Want It)
summary: Change isnвҖҷt hard because youвҖҷre lazy or вҖңnot disciplined enoughвҖқ. In this episode, weвҖҷre unpacking the real reason change feels so hard, even when you genuinely want it. If youвҖҷve ever started strong, then slid back into old habits and thought, what is wrong with me?вҖҰ this will give you a whole new lens. WeвҖҷll talk about why change isnвҖҷt just a decision or a fresh plan. It brings up discomfort, emotion, and fear because it challenges what your nervous system sees as вҖңsafeвҖқ. Your system is built to choose familiar over better, even when the familiar is draining you, because itвҖҷs predictable and it knows how to survive there. Then we go into the identity piece, the part most people miss. A lot of habits are tied to who you think you are, so changing the behaviour can feel like challenging your identity. IвҖҷll share the questions that actually help you move forward (without forcing), and how to shift from self-judgement into awareness so change becomes something you can sustain. title: 346  |  How to Stop Buying $h!t  -  And How Perfectionism and Self Worth Feed this Cycle
podcast_name: Declutter Your Chaos - Minimalism, Decluttering, Home Organization
summary: Hey Guys,  In this episode, I unpack the idea of outsourcing worthвҖ”the deeply learned pattern of looking outside ourselves for validation, safety, and permission to feel good. For many of us, this starts early, long before we ever think about buying things or organizing our homes.  I share a story that shaped how I understood this pattern: watching someone's confidence and radiance disappear the moment another person disapproved. When you grow up learning that someone else can "turn off the light," you adapt. You manage. You perform. And eventually, you start giving your power awayвҖ”to people, to perfection, and to stuff.  This is the hidden reason decluttering feels so charged.  Perfectionism, overbuying, and paralysis aren't character flaws. They're survival strategies rooted in fear and conditioning. The urge to replace the missing placemat, wait until everything is "just right," or avoid decluttering altogether isn't about aestheticsвҖ”it's about control and safety. title: How To Care For Someone With Dementia: Begin Again Moments
summary: What does it really feel like to lose someoneвҖҰ while theyвҖҷre still here? In this powerful Begin Again Moments, Jamie Theakston opens up about his fatherвҖҷs dementia, the quiet grief, the role reversal, and the heartbreak of watching a parent slowly change. He speaks candidly about the anxiety of caring for someone who wants to stay independent, and the complicated emotions that come with loving someone who is still physically present but gradually slipping away . Then psychologist Kimberley Wilson explains the science behind dementia and AlzheimerвҖҷs, including why women are at higher risk, the worrying rise in young-onset diagnoses, and the surprising lifestyle factors that could prevent up to 45% of cases. From hearing loss and social isolation to brain stimulation and stress, she shares what we can actually do to protect our brain health. This short episode features powerful clips from their full conversations, both available to watch or listen to now. If youвҖҷre caring for a parent, worried about your own risk, or navigating the complicated emotions of dementia, this conversation is for you. title: 150 | Vonn & Liu: Two Olympians, Two Comebacks. Grit Meets Freedom in Olympic Dreams
summary: About the Host Dr. Toby Brooks is the host of Becoming UnDoneВ®, a podcast that delves into the stories of high achievers who have navigated failures and setbacks on their path to success. With a strong background in performance science, Brooks is dedicated to helping individuals rebuild their identities and develop resilience through his app, Science of the Comeback. He is also actively engaged in personal coaching and engaging keynote speaking, offering insights drawn from his own experiences and those of his podcast guests. Episode Summary In this episode of Becoming UnDoneВ®, Dr. Toby Brooks shares his personal journey and deep love for the Olympics, reflecting on seminal moments such as the 1992 Olympics Triplecast and the impact sports have on shaping ambition and dreams. Drawing a parallel between the challenges faced by Olympians and personal comebacks, Brooks delves into the stories of Lindsey Vonn and Alysa Liu, two athletes whose paths to the Winter Olympics were marked by profound comebacks that offer rich insights into the nature of resilience, grit, and the motivation behind elite performance. Amidst a narrative of personal reflection and awe, Brooks examines how individuals like Lindsey Vonn exemplify relentless determination and grit, while Alysa Liu's journey is characterized by rediscovered joy and autonomy. By weaving their stories into the broader theme of becoming undone and then choosing a path towards reinvention, Brooks challenges listeners to question their own motivations. Highlighting the psychological differences in motivation, he presents a thought-provoking discourse on how one's purpose can redefine the journey of a comeback, urging listeners to reflect on their own narratives of achievement and identity. Key Takeaways The Power of Grit: The episode underscores how overcoming physical and emotional hurdles requires sheer determination, as shown by athletes like Lindsey Vonn.Embracing Autonomy: Alysa LiuвҖҷs story highlights the importance of reclaiming joy and autonomy, presenting a comeback fueled by personal choice.Identity and Purpose: Dr. Brooks discusses how high achievers often face identity foreclosure, where self-worth becomes narrowly tied to achievement.Purpose-Driven Motivation: The episode explores how intrinsic motivation, driven by mastery and meaning, can lead to sustainable joy.Reflective Growth: Listeners are encouraged to question their motivations for coming back from setbacks, emphasizing growth and self-discovery over external validation.Notable Quotes "Being great necessitates being unbalanced. You have to be unbalanced to find every bit of energy and strength that you have to pull it off." Reach out to Becoming UnDone! Text Toby here! title: Ep 538 - The worst stealth wealth destroyers
podcast_name: The Money To The Masses Podcast

title: If You DonвҖҷt See It, You CanвҖҷt Be It with Sinead Anderson
summary: This week on No More Booze, IвҖҷm joined by Sinead Anderson @anderson.sinead a Galway woman living in the south of France, whoвҖҷs three and a half years alcohol-free and wishes she had done it sooner. We chat about growing up shy, using booze for confidence and the years of itвҖҷs fine, everybody does it attitude  until the switch finally flipped.Sinead shares how quitting alongside her husband changed everything: no more shamey mornings, more self-acceptance and a real sense of reclaiming who she is. We talk visualisation,  using I donвҖҷt drink' as a full sentence, why moderation didnвҖҷt work and the new dopamine swaps (sea swimming and lifting weights).  Sinead is a life coach who's approach reflects that same grounded clarity. В She is based in France and works online and in person with her clients.В Gentle but attentive, she works with presence and care, listening deeply to what is saidвҖ”and to what might lie beneath the surface. I hope you enjoy listening to this episode as much as I did recording it.Find Sinead at sineadandersoncoaching.com and on Instagram @anderson.sinead To find out more about me, my work as an Alcohol-Free Coach & Mentor visit www.greyareadrinker.ie or find me on instagram @greyareadrinker

title: #38 Uncletics
summary: This week, Dave sits down with Leighton Clarke вҖ” better known as Uncletics вҖ” for a raw and honest kЕҚrero about owning who you are. From growing up in the King Country and navigating school with tourettes, to building a powerful online community without toning himself down for anyone вҖ” Leighton shares what itвҖҷs taken to fully back himself. They talk about stigma, being accused of faking it, online backlash, and why trying to please everyone is a losing game. Leighton doesnвҖҷt shrink himself to make others comfortable вҖ” and thatвҖҷs exactly why so many people connect with him. ItвҖҷs funny, confronting, heartfelt, and real. title: 356. Surviving the past and the power to change with Maddie from NSW
summary: *****TRIGGER WARNING***** This episode contains conversations describing domestic violence. Listener discretion is advised, please take care of yourself as you listen. In this episode, Maddie shares her profound journey from childhood experiences with alcohol to her struggles with addiction and the impact of family dynamics on her life. She recounts her early encounters with alcohol, the normalisation of substance use in her family, and the traumatic events that shaped her path. Maddie discusses the challenges of recovery, the search for identity, and the ongoing journey towards healing and reconciliation with her family. In this conversation, Maddie shares her profound journey through psychological abuse, addiction, and the path to sobriety. She reflects on her experiences with a verbally abusive partner, her struggles with alcohol, and the impact of her upbringing on her choices. Maddie discusses her transformation during pregnancy, the challenges of living in a small town, and the importance of community support in her recovery. She emphasises the significance of forgiveness, both for herself and her family, and the ongoing work required to heal from generational trauma. Ultimately, Maddie's story is one of resilience, self-discovery, and the power of change. For more resources such as coaching or to join the next HIQA challenge go to www.iquitalcohol.com.au Follow HIQA insta @howiquitalcoholВ  Music for Podcast intro and outro written by Danni Carr performed by Mr Cassidy If you are struggling with physical dependancy on alcohol consider contacting a local AA meeting or a drug and alcohol therapist.В Always consult a GP before stopping alcohol.В   Hosted on Acast. In this powerful episode, Henry Lawrence breaks down the truth most people avoid: your life reflects your identity. If youвҖҷre stuck, struggling, or battling resistance, itвҖҷs not punishment вҖ” itвҖҷs feedback. You donвҖҷt change your life by trying harder.You change it by becoming someone different. Inside this episode: How to rewrite your вҖңvibrational DNAвҖқ Why extreme belief changes outcomes The identity shift that makes reality respond How to stop arguing for your limitations A guided вҖңI AMвҖқ meditation to reprogram your self-image If youвҖҷre tired of effort without resultsвҖҰIf youвҖҷre ready to stop surviving and start creatingвҖҰThis is your wake-up call. Because when you shift your frequency вҖ”Life shifts with you. Listen all the way through for the deep identity-reset breathwork session. And if youвҖҷre serious about rewiring your mindset at the root, limited 1:1 coaching spots are open. This isnвҖҷt motivation.This is transformation.

title: I Thought Alcohol Was Helping My AnxietyвҖ”It WasnвҖҷt | MonnicaвҖҷs Naked Life | EP 882
summary: TRIGGER WARNING: This episode contains discussions of severe alcohol use disorder, blackout drinking, hospitalization, family trauma, and parental death. Using alcohol to calm downвҖ”does it actually work, or does it quietly make everything harder? Monnica didnвҖҷt start drinking to escape her life. She drank to manage it. What began as a way to settle postpartum nerves slowly turned into a cycle of binge drinking, blackouts, and fear. For years, Monnica believed alcohol was helping her cope with anxiety, stress, and overwhelmвҖ”until it stopped working altogether. In this episode, Monnica shares how undiagnosed anxiety shaped her relationship with alcohol, the wake-up calls that forced her to look closer, and how learning to regulate her nervous system helped her finally break free. In this episode, Monnica discusses: Starting to drink postpartum and using alcohol to calm down during stressful days as a stay-at-home mom Saving wine bottles and not recognizing the signs of alcohol addiction escalating Blackout drinking episodes leading to dangerous situations and memory loss The devastating night police were called after a confrontation with her son Waking up in the ER on her daughter's birthday after being found unconscious outside Walmart Finding This Naked Mind on Instagram and winning free access to The Path Learning about cognitive dissonance, giving alcohol a job, and the short chemical window of relief Understanding how childhood trauma and hidden material held her back The power of vulnerability, curiosity, and compassion in the recovery journey Receiving an Alive stone in June 2024 and experiencing a profound shift in her relationship with alcohol And more topicsвҖҰ Episode links: nakedmindpath.com Related Episodes: Why do I have so much anxiety the day after drinking? - Reader Question - E02 - https://thisnakedmind.com/ep-02-reader-question-much-anxiety-day-drinking/ Overcoming Alcohol-induced Anxiety - MarcusвҖҷ Naked Life - E607 - https://thisnakedmind.com/ep-607-naked-life-story-marcus-widblom/ Overcoming Drinking To Cope with Depression and Anxiety - SusanвҖҷs Naked Life - E681 https://thisnakedmind.com/ep-681-naked-life-story-susan-h/ Ready to take the next step on your journey?В Visit https://learn.thisnakedmind.com/podcast-resources for free resources, programs, and more. title: The Dark Tetrad - Epstein & Huge Dating Red Flags to Spot Early
summary: Start your 14-Day Free Trial to the All-Access Pass and keep the Master the Dating Stage of Relationships Course for Life as a free bonus. https://offer.personaldevelopmentschool.com/valentines-day?utm_source=podcast&utm_campaign=valentines-day&utm_medium=organic&utm_term=dDI_WYFEkdY&utm_content=pod-02-21-26&el=podcast The Dark Tetrad - Epstein & Huge Dating Red Flags to Spot Early. The Dark Tetrad refers to four socially harmful personality traits that can show up in dating, friendships, communities, and even positions of power: Narcissism Machiavellianism Psychopathy Sadism Episode Summary In this episode, Thais Gibson breaks down how these traits operate, how they overlap with grooming and manipulation dynamics, and most importantly, how to spot early warning signs before you learn the hard way. This isnвҖҷt about diagnosing people. ItвҖҷs about pattern recognition so you can protect your boundaries, your emotional health, and your future relationships. In todayвҖҷs video, youвҖҷll learn: вҖў What the Dark Tetrad is and why it matters in dating вҖў How narcissistic traits present early on вҖў The strategic manipulation behind Machiavellianism вҖў The emotional coldness and lack of remorse tied to psychopathy вҖў The disturbing enjoyment of othersвҖҷ discomfort seen in sadism YouвҖҷll also learn how to trust your bodyвҖҷs signals; confusion, anxiety, walking on eggshells as early indicators that something may not be right. Awareness is empowerment. Key Takeaways вң”пёҸThe difference between personality traits and clinical diagnoses вң”пёҸHow narcissists respond to boundaries and micro-disappointments вң”пёҸWhy Machiavellian individuals treat your вҖңnoвҖқ like a problem to solve вң”пёҸThe вҖңremorse testвҖқ for identifying psychopathic traits вң”пёҸHow sadistic tendencies can show up in dark humor, escalation, or cruelty вң”пёҸWhy chronic confusion and nervous system stress are red flags Timestamps 00:00 вҖ“ The Dark Tetrad 01:14 вҖ“ 1. title: 7 Signs YouвҖҷre Not in Control of Your Life | Ed Mylett
summary: What if the most dangerous thing in your life isnвҖҷt failureвҖҰ but the fact that youвҖҷre not the one driving? In this mashup episode, IвҖҷm bringing together two men who know what it feels like to lose control of their lives and then fight their way back into the driverвҖҷs seat. Darren Waller and Rod Carew open up about addiction, pressure, identity, faith, and the discipline it takes to reclaim your life when it starts spiraling. I share a story in this episode about a terrifying cab ride I took with my daughter. For 25 minutes, we were completely out of control. Someone else was driving recklessly, and we were just passengers in the back seat. That experience became a metaphor for something I see all the time. So many people are not actually driving their own lives. Their fears are driving. Their past is driving. Their addiction to approval is driving. Their anger, insecurity, comparison, or trauma is driving. And they are just along for the ride, hoping it does not end in a crash. Darren Waller talks about what it was like when addiction was behind the wheel of his life. From the outside, he was living the dream. Inside, he was losing control. He shares how recovery forced him to confront what was really driving him and how taking radical ownership changed everything. Rod Carew shares his journey from humble beginnings to Hall of Fame greatness, and how discipline, faith, and personal responsibility kept him focused when the spotlight and expectations could have easily taken over. This episode is about awareness. You cannot fix what you are unwilling to face. If you are reacting instead of choosing, if your emotions are dictating your behavior, if you keep repeating the same patterns and calling it bad luck, there is a good chance you are not in control. And the truth is, until you decide to take the wheel back, your life will keep zigging and zagging in ways you do not want. I want you walking away from this conversation asking yourself one powerful question: Who is driving my life right now? Because once you are honest about that, you can change it. You can grab the wheel. You can slow things down. You can choose a different direction. But you cannot do that if you stay in the back seat. Key Takeaways    The 7 signs you may not be in control of your own life    How fear silently dictates your decisions and limits your future    Darren WallerвҖҷs journey from addiction to elite performance    Rod CarewвҖҷs discipline blueprint for staying grounded in success    Why radical ownership is the first step to real freedom    How to move from being reactive to being intentional    The one question that can instantly shift your trajectory This is your wake up call. You were not born to sit in the back seat of your own life. title: Led by a Wolverine to Water: Adventures on the Trail with Wing Williams
summary: What if you could make a treaty with mosquitoesвҖҰ and they actually stopped biting you? In this episode, author and adventurer Wing Williams joins Brad Hook to share stories from his вҖңhowling twentiesвҖқ: thru-hiking the Appalachian Trail and Pacific Crest Trail, learning to walk through forests without bug spray or fear of poison ivy, following a wolverine to an unseen mountain spring вҖ” and eventually facing the darkness of addiction, spiritual warfare and a brutal near-death experience. Wing is the author of CINEREAL: A Memoir, a raw, poetic account of his life from wild adventure to the depths of alcoholism and spiritual torment, and finally to freedom in Jesus Christ. At 40, he now lives within the constraints of a traumatic brain injury while carrying a joy and clarity that only came after surrender. In this conversation we explore: The вҖңtreaty with mosquitoesвҖқ and what it taught Wing about presence and respect for the natural world Walking through poison ivy and poodle dog bush without reaction вҖ” and what our ancestors may have known that weвҖҷve forgotten How thru-hiking thousands of miles taught him to achieve massive goals through tiny, repeatable steps The hidden side of addiction: drinking to quiet demons, secret habits and the illusion of control The murder attempt that left him in the snow with a traumatic brain injury and a voice saying, вҖңMove or you will dieвҖқ How repeated detoxes, deep shame and relentless grace led him to finally surrender his life to Jesus What CINEREAL means, and why ashes, gray matter and rebirth all belong in his story Finding beauty even in the worst moments вҖ” and why a single ray of light can keep a soul alive If youвҖҷve ever chased adventure, battled addiction, wrestled with faith, or wondered how to keep walking when life feels impossible, this episode will stay with you.
